nodes.title: Default fallback keeps behavior predictable
nodes.why: If callers do not pass a fallback, `null` is a clear and consistent miss value.
nodes.alternative: Default to `undefined` or throw on parse failure.
nodes.tradeoff: `null` is explicit, but callers still need to handle null checks.

nodes.title: Guard non-string input first
nodes.why: This avoids unexpected behavior when input is not JSON text.
nodes.alternative: Call JSON.parse on everything and rely on exceptions.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra check, but clearer and safer behavior for mixed inputs.

nodes.title: Use native JSON.parse on valid strings
nodes.why: Built-in parsing is fast and standards-compliant for valid JSON.
nodes.tradeoff: Native parser is reliable, but throws on malformed JSON, so we need catch handling.

nodes.title: Catch malformed JSON safely
nodes.why: Invalid JSON should not crash callers using this helper.
nodes.tradeoff: Swallowing errors is convenient, but hides exact parse failure details.

nodes.title: Return the same fallback for all failure cases
nodes.why: A unified fallback path makes caller logic and tests straightforward.
nodes.alternative: Return different values for type mismatch vs parse failure.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple API, but less granular error information.
